nodes.title: Design matrix
nodes.statement: Observed design matrix X in R^{n x d}.

outgoing.description: The Gaussian observation model uses X as the design matrix.
The Student-t observation model uses X as the design matrix.

nodes.title: Response vector
nodes.statement: Observed response vector y in R^n.

outgoing.description: The Gaussian observation model defines the distribution of y.
The Student-t observation model defines the conditional distribution of y.

nodes.title: Regression coefficients
nodes.statement: Unknown coefficient vector beta in R^d.

outgoing.description: Beta is the unknown coefficient vector in the observation model.
The Gaussian prior is a distributional assumption on beta.
Beta is the unknown coefficient vector in the Student-t observation model.

nodes.title: Noise vector
nodes.statement: Noise vector epsilon in R^n.

outgoing.description: The Gaussian observation model assigns a Gaussian distribution to the noise vector epsilon.
The Student-t observation model assigns a heavy-tailed distribution to the noise vector epsilon.

nodes.title: Noise scale
nodes.statement: Positive noise scale sigma in R_{>0}.

outgoing.description: The Gaussian observation model uses sigma as the positive noise scale.
The Student-t observation model uses sigma as the positive scale parameter.

nodes.title: Prior scale
nodes.statement: Positive prior scale tau in R_{>0}.

outgoing.description: The Gaussian prior uses tau as its positive prior scale.

nodes.title: Student-t degrees of freedom
nodes.statement: Positive Student-t degrees of freedom nu in R_{>0}.

outgoing.description: The Student-t observation model uses nu as the positive degrees-of-freedom parameter.

nodes.title: Gaussian linear observation model
nodes.displayLabel: y = Xβ + ε, ε ~ N(0, σ²I)
nodes.statement: y = X beta + epsilon, epsilon ~ N(0, sigma^2 I).

incoming.description: The Gaussian observation model uses X as the design matrix.
The Gaussian observation model defines the distribution of y.
Beta is the unknown coefficient vector in the observation model.
The Gaussian observation model assigns a Gaussian distribution to the noise vector epsilon.
The Gaussian observation model uses sigma as the positive noise scale.

outgoing.description: The Gaussian observation model implies the Gaussian likelihood.

nodes.title: Gaussian prior on beta
nodes.statement: beta ~ N(0, tau^2 I_d).

incoming.description: The Gaussian prior is a distributional assumption on beta.
The Gaussian prior uses tau as its positive prior scale.

outgoing.description: The Gaussian prior is required for the closed-form posterior.
The Student-t MAP approximation keeps the Gaussian prior on beta.

nodes.title: Gaussian likelihood
nodes.statement: p(y | X, beta, sigma^2) under iid Gaussian noise.

incoming.description: The Gaussian observation model implies the Gaussian likelihood.

outgoing.description: Combining the Gaussian likelihood with the Gaussian prior yields the conjugate Gaussian posterior.

nodes.title: Closed-form Gaussian posterior
nodes.statement: p(beta | X,y) = N(mu_n, Sigma_n).

incoming.description: Combining the Gaussian likelihood with the Gaussian prior yields the conjugate Gaussian posterior.
The Gaussian prior is required for the closed-form posterior.

outgoing.description: The Gaussian posterior induces a quadratic negative log posterior objective.
The MAP estimator is the posterior mode. For the Gaussian posterior it equals the posterior mean.

nodes.title: Gaussian negative log posterior
nodes.statement: Quadratic objective equivalent to the Gaussian MAP estimator.

incoming.description: The Gaussian posterior induces a quadratic negative log posterior objective.

outgoing.description: Minimizing the Gaussian negative log posterior gives the MAP estimator.

nodes.title: MAP estimator for beta
nodes.statement: The MAP estimator minimizes the Gaussian negative log posterior and equals the posterior mean in the conjugate Gaussian model.

incoming.description: Minimizing the Gaussian negative log posterior gives the MAP estimator.
The MAP estimator is the posterior mode. For the Gaussian posterior it equals the posterior mean.

outgoing.description: The synthetic recovery validation checks whether the estimator approaches the true coefficient vector as n increases.

nodes.title: Synthetic recovery validation
nodes.statement: Empirical validation that posterior mean/MAP estimation error decreases as sample size increases under data generated from the Gaussian model.

incoming.description: The synthetic recovery validation checks whether the estimator approaches the true coefficient vector as n increases.

outgoing.description: The experiment executes the synthetic recovery validation.

nodes.title: Gaussian recovery experiment
nodes.statement: Runs synthetic recovery experiment over multiple sample sizes and seeds.

incoming.description: The experiment executes the synthetic recovery validation.

nodes.title: Student-t noise observation model
nodes.displayLabel: y = Xβ + ε, ε ~ t_ν(0, σ)
nodes.statement: y = X beta + epsilon, epsilon ~ StudentT_nu(0, sigma).

incoming.description: The Student-t observation model uses X as the design matrix.
The Student-t observation model defines the conditional distribution of y.
Beta is the unknown coefficient vector in the Student-t observation model.
The Student-t observation model assigns a heavy-tailed distribution to the noise vector epsilon.
The Student-t observation model uses sigma as the positive scale parameter.
The Student-t observation model uses nu as the positive degrees-of-freedom parameter.

outgoing.description: The Student-t observation model implies the Student-t likelihood.

nodes.title: Student-t likelihood
nodes.statement: Student-t likelihood for robust regression.

incoming.description: The Student-t observation model implies the Student-t likelihood.

outgoing.description: Since conjugacy is not used here, the estimator is implemented as a numerical MAP approximation.

nodes.title: Numerical MAP approximation under Student-t noise
nodes.statement: MAP objective optimized numerically using a Student-t likelihood and Gaussian prior; this is not a closed-form posterior.

incoming.description: Since conjugacy is not used here, the estimator is implemented as a numerical MAP approximation.
The Student-t MAP approximation keeps the Gaussian prior on beta.

outgoing.description: The Student-t change experiment exercises the numerical MAP approximation.

nodes.title: Student-t change experiment
nodes.statement: Compares Gaussian MAP and Student-t MAP under heavy-tailed noise.

incoming.description: The Student-t change experiment exercises the numerical MAP approximation.

nodes.title: \(F \in \mathcal{P}_{\mathrm{fin}}(\mathrm{Path})\)
nodes.statement: F is the finite set of repository paths considered during initialization.

outgoing.description: The finite repository corpus is partitioned by authority and provenance.

nodes.title: \(F=A\,\dot\cup\,G\,\dot\cup\,E\)
nodes.statement: Repository evidence is partitioned into authoritative files, aggregate generated families, and explicit exclusions using defaults plus project configuration.

incoming.description: The finite repository corpus is partitioned by authority and provenance.

outgoing.description: Classification determines which records receive semantic extraction and which become aggregate manifests.

nodes.title: \(I=\mathcal{H}(A)\cup\mathcal{T}(G)\)
nodes.statement: The initialization index stores semantic records for authoritative evidence and hash/provenance manifests for aggregate families.

incoming.description: Classification determines which records receive semantic extraction and which become aggregate manifests.

outgoing.description: The authoritative index records generate the compact dossier.
Indexed scopes and occurrences generate exact role-classified references.
Hash and coverage checks validate initialization traceability.

nodes.title: \(D=\operatorname{compact}(I_A)\)
nodes.statement: The dossier deduplicates equations, definitions, calls, tests, defaults, and identity candidates from authoritative evidence.

incoming.description: The authoritative index records generate the compact dossier.

nodes.title: \(R_s=\{(p,\ell,r):s\text{ occurs at }(p,\ell)\}\)
nodes.statement: Reference drafting emits exhaustive authoritative path-line occurrences with deterministic semantic roles and graph candidates.

incoming.description: Indexed scopes and occurrences generate exact role-classified references.

nodes.title: \(\operatorname{valid}(I)\iff\operatorname{current}(I)\land\operatorname{complete}(I_A)\)
nodes.statement: Validation checks current hashes, complete authoritative coverage, grouped generated evidence, shared derivations, and render isolation.

incoming.description: Hash and coverage checks validate initialization traceability.

outgoing.description: Validated traceability data and render isolation generate the interactive static graph viewer.

nodes.title: \(V=\operatorname{render}(G_{\mathrm{mg}},L_{\mathrm{SCC}},B)\)
nodes.statement: The graph viewer renders the validated mathgraph with SCC-aware layout, measured node boxes, and interactive traceability links.

incoming.description: Validated traceability data and render isolation generate the interactive static graph viewer.

edges.description: The finite repository corpus is partitioned by authority and provenance.
Classification determines which records receive semantic extraction and which become aggregate manifests.
The authoritative index records generate the compact dossier.
Indexed scopes and occurrences generate exact role-classified references.
Hash and coverage checks validate initialization traceability.
Validated traceability data and render isolation generate the interactive static graph viewer.
